title: Popcorn
author: Ben Elton

_TBC_from_dean.running_time_minutes: Estimated 130; confirm with Dean post-tech run.
_TBC_from_dean.interval_minutes: Assumed 20; confirm with Dean and bar team.

_image_notes: image_local is the canonical reference for foyer-pack. If David's own Popcorn.jpg differs from the site image, drop it at assets/posters/popcorn-2026.jpg to override — download-assets.py will skip an existing file unless --force is passed.
synopsis: A play adaptation of Ben Elton's 1996 novel. Hollywood film director Bruce Delamitri's Oscar-night celebrations are cut short when two violent fans, claiming his films inspired their crimes, invade his home. A sharp, darkly comic thriller about art, celebrity and responsibility in a culture addicted to spectacle and violence.
_tagline_full: Art, celebrity, responsibility — and what we'll forgive for a good story. (Shortened 2026-06-10: the about-play slide headlines the tagline in caps and the long form overflowed the slide.)

foyer_content._notes: Engine slide content (FOY-01b templates read these). Text + image paths ported 2026-06-10 from the signed-off design source popcorn-foyer.html, which remains the design reference.

writer.body: Stand-up comedian, novelist, screenwriter. Co-creator of The Young Ones and Blackadder; writer of Popcorn as a novel in 1996 and as the stage play that has toured since 2000. The play won the Olivier Award for Best New Comedy.

director.quote: Ben Elton asked in 1996 who's to blame when our culture feeds on violence. Thirty years and a billion screens later, the question hasn't aged — it's multiplied. That's why we're staging Popcorn now.
director._TBC_from_dean: Placeholder quote (David-drafted). Replace with Dean's 25-40 word note when received — see placeholders.director-quote.

trivia.headline: The Oscars
trivia.body: The Academy Awards have been broadcast on television every year since 1953. The first ceremony, in 1929, lasted just 15 minutes. The winners had been announced in the newspapers three months earlier — so it was less a competition than a victory dinner.

venue.body: The Criterion has staged over 450 productions since 1955. Two of them were world premieres. Members have played more than 6,000 parts and crewed 8,000 backstage roles. The building you're standing in is over 140 years old — an 1884 Methodist chapel that became a theatre in 1960.
venue.footline: 200 Company members • 400 supporting members • 0 paid employees

features._notes: In-world poster blurbs for the popcorn-feature template, keyed by titleFromAsset of the poster filename. Ordinary Americans + The Unamerican ported from the signed-off design source; Mall Murderers drafted 2026-06-10 (David to approve).

ordinary-americans.body: The Oscar-winning film at the heart of tonight's story. Bruce Delamitri's blood-soaked portrait of small-town America — and the movie two killers swear taught them everything.
ordinary-americans.footline: "Some people call it violence. He calls it truth."

the-unamerican.body: Hollywood's most dangerous director, in his own words. The magazine profile behind Bruce Delamitri's most explosive masterpiece — too slick, too violent, too honest?

mall-murderers.body: The killing spree America couldn't stop watching. Wayne and Scout — celebrity outlaws in a culture addicted to the spectacle — claim Bruce Delamitri's films wrote their script.

brooke-daniels.body: Model, actress, and the face on every newsstand. Brooke Daniels wants to be taken seriously in a town that only takes pictures — and tonight, on Oscar night, she's about to land the role of her life.

content_warnings.headline: Loud gunshot effects
content_warnings.detail: Sudden loud noises (gunshot effects) during Act II.

content_warnings.headline: Strong language
content_warnings.detail: Features frequent strong language, offensive slurs, and sexual terms.

content_warnings.headline: Age guidance 15+
content_warnings.detail: Recommended for ages 15 and above. The Criterion does not enforce age but advises caution.

content_warnings.headline: Simulated violence
content_warnings.detail: Includes scenes of hostage-taking, physical threats, and guns used to intimidate people.

content_warnings.headline: Mature themes
content_warnings.detail: Adult themes around media violence, celebrity culture, and the sexualisation of crime.

content_warnings.headline: References to suicide and self-harm
content_warnings.detail: Verbal references to suicide and self-harm. No on-stage depiction.
content_warnings._TBC_from_dean: Confirm whether Dean's cut retains these specific lines.

content_warnings.headline: Flashing lights
content_warnings.detail: Camera flashes and rapid light changes during the Oscar-night and TV-studio sequences.
content_warnings._TBC_from_olivia: Confirm with Olivia whether her LX design uses strobing.

phase_policy.audio_allowed_note: Per 01-research-and-capabilities.md §B: silent-first for Popcorn. Burnt-in open captions required on any video that contains speech.

accessibility_policy.principle: Design for everyone, don't make disabled patrons ask. Stagetext convention.

video_captions._notes: All spoken-word video shown on the foyer screen must carry burnt-in open captions. obs-helper should refuse to play a video flagged as containing speech but lacking captions.verified=true (enforcement to follow in Phase 2; convention recorded now).

accessibility_policy.contrast_minimum: WCAG AAA for body text on dark backgrounds
accessibility_policy.flash_rate_note: Foyer screen content itself never exceeds 3Hz flash. Strobing in the show is warned for separately under content_warnings.flashing-lights.
accessibility_policy.colour_information_redundancy: Information must not be conveyed by colour alone. Content warnings carry text labels; CTAs carry words, not just neon hue.

_auth_comment: Dev passcode hashes preserved from pre-import stub show.json. The new David-authored show.json did not include these and login was failing 403. Rotate before any non-dev deployment using auth.pbkdf2_hash() in platform/obs-operator/auth.py. Current values map to: operator -> 'operator-passcode', cue-trigger -> 'cue-trigger-passcode', foh -> 'foh-passcode'.
